description_long: This is a World War II-era twin-fuselage military airplane with a distinctive camouflage paint scheme of brown and gray, featuring Royal Air Force roundels on the wings and fuselage. The aircraft has two propeller-driven engines mounted on the front of each fuselage, with a central cockpit canopy connecting the two bodies. The tail section is joined by a horizontal stabilizer, and the vertical stabilizers are positioned at the end of each fuselage. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels under each fuselage and a tail wheel at the rear. The airplane's surface appears metallic with painted markings, and it is detailed with antenna wires and visible cockpit framing.
description: A twin-fuselage, propeller-driven military airplane with camouflage paint, RAF roundels, and a central cockpit canopy. Features include fixed landing gear, dual engines, and a joined tail section.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing both propellers, cockpit canopy, and the RAF roundels on the wings.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the twin tail structure, horizontal stabilizer, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the tail assembly, both vertical stabilizers, and the camouflage pattern.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the propellers, cockpit canopy, and wing roundels.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets, slight dihedral angle, and twin-fuselage layout.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted at the front of each fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a connecting horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and two tail wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Central, forward-mounted enclosed cockpit canopy connecting the two fuselages, likely multi-seat.
